v1_definition: Curio v1.0 is a publicly-announceable, single-machine, local-first power reader: TWO shipped heads over one Tauri-free `curio-core` crate — (1) `curio-desktop` (Tauri 2 + Svelte 5 runes) on macOS 13+ (arm64 + x64, signed + notarized DMGs), Windows 10+ (x64, NSIS + MSI, Authenticode-signed or with a documented SmartScreen fallback decision), Linux (AppImage with updater + deb/rpm with updater compiled out, WebKitGTK 4.1 documented); (2) `curio-cli` (cargo install / brew tap), the agent/cron/scripting surface and CI proof the core is headless. Feature bar: articles-only reading done excellently — 1000-feed policy-hardened fetch engine (SSRF guard, size/redirect caps, conditional GET), sanitize-at-ingest pipeline (ammonia → readability → populated FTS5), event-sourced read/star/read-later state, OPML import AND export (curio:* round-trip), instant full-text search at 100k articles, keyboard-first triage (full j/k/g-chord map), light/dark/system + the 9 ported token themes, image cache (XDG-correct, LRU-capped), and the two strategic contracts as core modules: generic markdown-vault export (htmd CommonMark + templated YAML frontmatter carrying curio_id UUIDv7 + sha256 checksum + schema_version — a near-drop-in for RFC-0018's checksum-keyed inbox contract, but fully generic) with named destinations, in-vault .curio/manifest.json idempotency, and atomic writes; plus the append-only reading-events JSONL stream (file sink with rotation/cursor + webhook sink) with published, semver'd JSON Schema artifacts and human-readable frontmatter-v1.md/events-v1.md contracts. Quality bar to announce (all CI-gated, not claimed): cold start < 1.5s to interactive on a 50k-article fixture on CI runners; 1000-feed wiremock refresh < 30s on M-series / < 60s on CI; FTS p95 < 50ms @100k; 500-feed nasty corpus parses without panic; stored-XSS fixture provably inert end-to-end; zero hand-written IPC (tauri-specta bindings CI-diffed); ≥85% region coverage on curio-core; 24h fuzz-clean; 14 consecutive dogfood-beta days at 300+ real feeds with zero P0/P1. Trust bar: dual MIT/Apache-2.0, cargo-deny enforced, zero telemetry with PRIVACY.md enumerating every network-call class, SECURITY.md + private vulnerability reporting, signed updater with key-custody/rotation ADR, SBOM + provenance + minisign on every release, docs site with per-platform install + the vault/events integration guide + migration guides, README with the files-are-the-API frame and a verbatim honest "what v1 does NOT do" sync section (no multi-device state sync; the vault/git path and `curio export --all` are what cross machines; server-head and client-mode are the named future seams). Explicitly NOT in v1: sync of any kind, Reddit/YouTube enrichment (layouts only), podcasts, profiles, theme editor, server head. Honest total: ~28 weeks (6.5–8 months) for one developer with heavy AI-agent leverage across 6 phases — versus the old PRD's 17–23 weeks, which budgeted zero for release engineering, and the validation report's "Phase-1 ~70%" which was really ~35–40% salvage-by-relocation.

phases.name: Phase 0 — Pre-public reset & workspace skeleton
phases.goal: Settle every irreversible-after-first-artifact decision (name, identifier, license, git history, updater keys) and lay the core-plus-heads Cargo workspace with the core/UI boundary mechanically enforced before any feature code exists.
deliverables: Name/trademark clearance vs Zengobi Curio et al. + owned domain; reverse-DNS bundle identifier replacing com.curio-reader.app (no hyphen, no .app suffix); scrub stray 'Flowfeed' branding — recorded as an ADR

History purge while still private: git filter-repo removes the 33MB committed yt-dlp binary; gitignore src-tauri/bin/ + src-tauri/gen/; LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE at root with SPDX in both manifests; brand-assets marks policy

Branch reconciliation: cherry-pick the Cfqby branch's two ideas (frontend build before clippy — tauri::generate_context! validates frontendDist/icons at compile time — and real icons/trimmed gen schemas) into the new baseline; delete both claude/* branches; commit a salvage manifest of what ports (error.rs, models, DDL→migration-0001 basis, parser.rs, opml.rs, ammonia config, YouTube/Reddit URL parsers + tests, app.css tokens + 9 themes, AddFeedModal a11y pattern, format.ts, lefthook, .vscode, CI bones) and what dies (regex markdown.rs, Mutex<Connection> storage, sequential refresh, player.ts, podcast dead structs, ts-rs scaffolding, mockall/tokio-test/image/@playwright/@testing-library/plugin-shell-JS dead deps)

Cargo + npm workspace: crates/curio-core, crates/curio-types, crates/curio-cli, apps/desktop, fixtures/, xtask/; workspace lints (clippy correctness/suspicious/perf deny, unwrap_used+expect_used deny outside tests); deny.toml with tauri BANNED from curio-core's dependency tree; curio-server demoted to docs/design/server-head.md ONLY per judge graft — nothing unshipped has to keep compiling

Governance/docs skeletons: SECURITY.md (GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting enabled), PRIVACY.md (enumerated network-call classes), GOVERNANCE.md ('personal-first, community-welcome' + wanted/out-of-scope contributions), CONTRIBUTING.md fronted by the Tauri-on-Linux system-deps story + devcontainer + nix flake, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, DCO, issue templates (bug-with-diagnostics-bundle, feed-compatibility-report that feeds the corpus, feature), PR template with conventional-commit rule + manual-smoke checklist slot

Long-lead credentials started NOW (weeks of latency, gate nothing else): Apple Developer Program enrollment + Developer ID cert + App Store Connect API key for notarytool; Azure Trusted Signing eligibility check (fallback: Certum OSS cert); tauri updater minisign keypair generated OFFLINE with key-custody + rotation/compromise ADR (transitional-release rotation scheme documented before it's needed)
CUT here from old PRD: 'Flowfeed' branding, com.curio-reader.app identifier, committed yt-dlp binary, OPML-files-as-profiles model, husky remnants, all four dead Rust deps
acceptance: git history contains zero blobs > 1MB (rev-list object-size check in CI)
A canary PR adding tauri as a curio-core dependency fails CI via cargo-deny; `cargo test --workspace` runs green on a fresh Linux clone with NO webview system deps installed for core/types/cli
LICENSE files present; identifier is reverse-DNS of an owned domain; name-clearance ADR merged
devcontainer and nix flake each boot to a passing `cargo test -p curio-core` on Linux (verified by a clean-machine run)
All governance docs exist and pass lint; private vulnerability reporting toggled on

phases.name: Phase 1 — Production scaffolding: CI/CD, release pipeline, test substrate
phases.goal: The OSS production layer BEFORE features: every gate, the signed draft-release pipeline, and the perf/correctness harness that all subsequent code is built against — releasing from the first alpha so the pipeline hardens with the product instead of being a 1.0-week scramble.
deliverables: PR gate suite on one cheap Linux runner (the 2-year CI-economics graft): cargo fmt --check, clippy --workspace -D warnings, cargo-nextest, MSRV job, biome ci + eslint-plugin-svelte (biome alone can't lint Svelte templates) + svelte-check --fail-on-warnings, vitest; the ipc-contract job (regenerate tauri-specta bindings, git diff --exit-code — the single highest-value gate, kills the snake_case/error-drift class by construction); cargo-deny ENFORCED; dependency-review gate; all actions SHA-pinned; permissions contents:read; concurrency-cancel

Merge-to-main/nightly workflows (not per-PR): 3-OS `npm run tauri build` matrix (ubuntu-22.04 pinning WebKitGTK 4.1 + webkit deps, macos arm64 + x64, windows) with signing conditional on secrets so fork PRs pass; nightly cargo-audit + osv-scanner (Rust+npm) + cargo-machete + zizmor + CodeQL (rust + js-ts); criterion vs stored baseline via github-action-benchmark (130% alert threshold, results to gh-pages — the future public benchmark page)

Release pipeline live from v0.1.0-alpha.1: release-please (conventional-commits) + tauri-action producing a DRAFT release with human-gated publish; single version source (package.json → tauri.conf version pointer → set-version.mjs syncing Cargo.toml); artifacts: per-arch DMGs, NSIS + MSI, AppImage (updater-enabled) + deb/rpm (updater compiled out); SHA256SUMS + minisign signature + CycloneDX SBOM + actions/attest-build-provenance on every release; TAURI_SIGNING secrets scoped to the tag-triggered workflow only

Test substrate before feature code: curio-fixtures crate with deterministic seeded generator (1000 feeds / 50k articles / realistic multi-line HTML bodies) shared by benches, integration tests, and manual QA seeding; wiremock fetch-pipeline harness; nasty-feed corpus skeleton (synthesized/scrubbed for clean licensing); cargo-fuzz targets scaffolded with seed corpora (fuzz_feed_parse, fuzz_opml_parse, fuzz_content_pipeline asserting no <script/on*= output ever, fuzz_url_parsers); criterion bench skeletons (parse-1k-item feed, batch-upsert-10k, FTS @100k, keyset page fetch); cargo-llvm-cov wiring with the honest split (core gated 85%, storage/commands/frontend report-only Codecov flags)

Renovate config: grouped weekly minor/patch PRs, immediate security PRs, GitHub Actions digest pinning, monthly lockfile maintenance, custom regex manager reserved for the future yt-dlp version+SHA pin

CUT here from old PRD/brief: Playwright-drives-Tauri E2E and window.__TAURI_TEST_RESET__ hooks (unimplementable/backdoor — replaced by mockIPC + tauri-driver + manual matrix); blanket 80/90% coverage gates and the nursery cognitive-complexity clippy gate (replaced by core-only 85% + workspace lints)
acceptance: Tagging v0.1.0-alpha.1 produces a draft release containing all artifact classes + SHA256SUMS + minisign sig + SBOM + provenance; the macOS DMG notarizes and staples (spctl + stapler verified in the workflow)
A simulated fork PR (secrets absent) passes the entire PR gate suite green with unsigned artifacts
Fixture generator determinism: same seed → byte-identical SQLite file, hash-asserted in CI
All 4 fuzz targets run 10 min nightly crash-free on seed corpora; the corpus runner executes end-to-end on the skeleton corpus
PR CI wall-clock < 10 minutes with warm caches (Swatinem/rust-cache + npm cache + built-frontend cache keyed on src hash)

phases.name: Phase 2 — curio-core reading engine (store, fetch, ingest, pipeline)
phases.goal: Build the hard 20% of an RSS reader as a headless, embeddable, plain-cargo-test-able crate: policy-hardened fetching, real-world-robust parsing, sanitize-at-ingest content pipeline, pooled WAL SQLite with event-sourced article state, and FTS that actually indexes content — killing every scale/safety defect the dossier found at the root.
deliverables: store/: numbered embedded migrations (transactional, forward-only, automatic pre-migration backup; migration 0001 = the branch's good DDL amended: INTEGER PRIMARY KEY rowid alias + UUIDv7 unique column for stable FTS external-content mapping, article_state/events split out of articles, single RFC3339 date format, FTS trigger scoped to content changes only); dedicated writer thread + read pool behind an async repo facade (spawn_blocking inside — heads never see rusqlite, the blocking-in-async bug class can't recur); escaped FTS5 query builder (never raw MATCH); keyset pagination everywhere; SQL-owned counts; full ArticleFilter honored (all 10 fields); network-filesystem guard refusing Syncthing/iCloud/NFS-class mounts without an explicit override flag; startup integrity_check + recovery path; retention policy that can never reap starred/read-later/exported items

fetch/: one mandatory PolicedClient for ALL outbound requests — deny-resolve loopback/RFC1918/link-local (SSRF), redirect cap 5 with per-hop policy re-check, streaming-enforced size caps (feed 10MB / image 20MB / favicon 1MB), 10s connect / 30s total timeouts, shared pooled client, conditional GET with etag/last_modified PRESERVED on error paths (fixing the clobber bug), permanent-redirect stored-URL updates, 410 dead-feed auto-pause, honest User-Agent, billion-laughs fixture test on the XML path

ingest/: feed-rs parse → normalize (dedupe chain guid → link → hash(title+published) as a total function, published→updated date fallbacks, xml:base relative-URL resolution, JSON Feed support, site_url rel=self fix) → ammonia sanitize (the ported config, finally actually called) → readability extraction → content_text population (FTS stops being a silent no-op); char-boundary-safe truncation (the UTF-8 panic becomes a permanent proptest)

pipeline/: the fixed refresh.rs design as the ONLY fetch path (sequential command duplicate deleted): bounded buffer_unordered, per-feed backoff + health tracking, scheduler, batch single-transaction INSERT..ON CONFLICT upserts (replacing 2N statements + N fsyncs), progress events; validated settings (fetch_concurrency ≥ 1, refresh_interval floor)

events/ write path: append-only events table (subscribed/read/starred/read_later/promoted) as THE state-change mechanism with article_state as a projection; cursor semantics designed now (`events since <cursor>`) so JSONL export, CLI tail, and a future server head's GET /v1/events?since= share one cursor — the cheapest down-payment on the server head (judge graft)

Ports landed: error.rs three-tier model, serde model layer (duplicate Theme structs deleted, aspirational no-op settings removed), opml.rs curio:* round-trip, fixed folder-tree algorithm (topologically ordered, the vanishing-subtree bug gets a regression test), enrich/ trait ports defined with zero providers

Test walls: wiremock integration suite (304 short-circuit, timeout, redirect cap, size cap, etag-preserved-on-error, permanent redirect, 410 pause); proptest suites (dedupe totality, date normalization always RFC3339 UTC, FTS builder never syntax-errors on arbitrary input, truncation never panics); concurrent reader+writer WAL tests; migration tests against fixture DBs; writer-thread stress test with the pre-agreed fallback decision point (tokio-rusqlite/sqlx) written down before any crisis
CUT here from old PRD: profiles schema (no profile_id anywhere — post-v1 as SQLite-first), Reddit/YouTube/podcast enrichment paths (trait ports only), smart folders, feed statistics
acceptance: 1000-feed fixture full refresh via wiremock: < 30s on an M-series dev machine, < 60s on the CI runner (bench-gated)

Corpus of ≥500 real-world-shaped feeds (wrong/undeclared encodings, invalid UTF-8, RSS 1.0/RDF, HTML soup, missing/duplicate GUIDs, timezone-less and garbage dates, xml:base, itunes/podcast-2.0 namespaces) parses without panic, and ≥95% of items yield non-empty content_text
batch-upsert-10k: one transaction (statement-count asserted), < 1s on the CI baseline; FTS query @100k articles p95 < 50ms on CI runner
A read query issued mid-refresh returns p95 < 100ms (concurrent reader/writer test) — the UI-freeze class is dead
Read/star state flips do NOT touch the FTS index (trigger assertion test)
The <script>/onerror hostile fixture ingests to provably clean stored HTML — the dossier's CRITICAL stored-XSS gets its permanent regression test
cargo-llvm-cov ≥ 85% region on curio-core; all fuzz targets 4h cumulative crash-free; curio-core still builds with zero tauri/UI deps (cargo-deny gate green)

phases.name: Phase 3 — Vault export + event log (the strategic seam) + curio-cli
phases.goal: Ship the generic markdown-vault exporter and reading-signals event stream as core modules with published, versioned schemas — simultaneously the strongest OSS differentiator (the free, local, deterministic Readwise→Obsidian pipeline) and the Knowledge Plane's ingestion contract (RFC-0018 checksum-keyed frontmatter), with zero KP-specific code — plus the CLI head that proves the core runs headless and gives agents/cron a surface.
deliverables: export/: htmd DOM-walk HTML→CommonMark (replacing the regex converter that destroyed multi-line elements) with a real-world fixture suite; minijinja frontmatter templates — default fields title, author, source URL, feed, published, saved, tags, curio_id (UUIDv7), checksum (sha256 of normalized body), schema_version — deliberately mirroring RFC-0018's checksum-for-dedupe/incremental-reindex key while staying fully generic ('any directory of Markdown + YAML'); per-feed/per-tag routing rules to subdirectories; optional vault-relative image assets

Named destination registry (server-first graft): promotion is always 'promote article X to destination NAME' — raw filesystem paths never cross IPC or CLI flags; destinations registered once via dialog pick (desktop) or config (CLI); every write canonicalizes and asserts containment; structurally kills the arbitrary-file-write class

Idempotency manifest dual-homed: SQLite table + in-vault .curio/manifest.json (curio_id → vault-relative path + last checksum) so a wiped-and-reinstalled Curio pointed at an existing vault RECONCILES instead of re-duplicating (monolith graft); deterministic slug-collision suffixes; atomic tmp+rename writes (the vault is git-/watcher-observed mid-write); documented two-machines-one-synced-vault conflict recovery path

events/ read side: JSONL projection {schema_version, event, article{curio_id,url,title,feed}, ts}; configurable file sink with daily rotation + cursor checkpointing (pointable at a synced folder or the vault's .curio/events/); webhook sink kind (push-capable for automation users and the KP librarian); `curio events tail` and `events since <cursor>` over the shared cursor

curio-types contracts: schemars → JSON Schema artifacts (frontmatter-v1, events-v1) published per release, PLUS human-readable docs/schemas/frontmatter-v1.md and events-v1.md with an explicit written breaking-change policy (additive in minors, breaking only in majors) — prose contracts survive tooling churn; a mandatory schema-review gate before first external consumer wiring, because the KP becomes consumer #1 immediately and a wrong identity key is a real breaking change

curio-cli v1 surface: add / fetch / query / search / export / events tail / opml import+export; `curio export --all` one-command full-portability archive (OPML + events JSONL + settings + read-later) — the anti-lock-in pitch and second-machine seeding story in one command; installable via cargo install and brew tap; wired as the daily headless E2E job in CI (any Tauri leakage breaks this build)

CUT here from old PRD: 'Obsidian vault integration' as a Phase-5 branded feature (promoted here as the generic contract); batch-export combined-file-with-separators and Base64Embed image mode (per-article files + LocalCopy only); hardcoded 5-string frontmatter match (replaced by templates)
acceptance: Idempotency: exporting the same article twice → zero file writes (manifest hit); content edit → in-place update; two same-title articles → distinct deterministic slugs (all as tests)
Wipe-and-reinstall integration test: delete the SQLite DB, fresh install pointed at the existing vault, re-promotion reconciles against .curio/manifest.json with zero duplicate notes
500-article corpus export: 100% of notes parse as valid YAML + CommonMark; every emitted note validates against frontmatter-v1 JSON Schema in CI; every JSONL line validates against events-v1
proptest: adversarial {title} filename templates never escape the vault root; simulated mid-write crash leaves no partial note visible

Generic-consumer proof: a standalone script with no Curio code ingests the exported vault + tails the JSONL and reconstructs read/star state — run against a scratch KP-shaped inbox/ as the acceptance demo (KP integration is thereafter configuration only)

`cargo install curio-cli` on a webview-less machine: add → fetch (wiremock) → search → export → events tail all function; this exact flow is the CI headless E2E job; asciinema of it recorded as a README asset

phases.name: Phase 4 — Desktop head: the v1 reader
phases.goal: The thin Tauri 2 + Svelte 5 runes head over Arc<CurioCore> — the surface users actually judge — with IPC types generated (drift impossible by construction), a strict security posture replacing csp:null/no-capabilities, and the old PRD's five-view-mode breadth cut to an excellent articles-first reader with layout-only rich views.
deliverables: tauri-specta generated TS bindings + error types, committed and CI-diffed, hand-written invoke() lint-banned (specta version pinned against Tauri 2.x coupling risk; committed bindings mean builds never depend on generator availability); ~40 thin commands delegating to Arc<CurioCore> with zero business logic; settings loaded at startup; the ported three-tier error model over the generated contract

Security posture: strict CSP (default-src 'self'; frame-src youtube-nocookie.com; connect-src ipc:; no unsafe-eval); capabilities/main.json per-window ACLs (currently absent entirely); tauri-plugin-shell removed; tauri-plugin-opener with URL scope for open-in-browser (fixing the broken window.open); ipc_policy module — export/import paths accepted only from dialog picks or the destination registry, never raw IPC strings; single sanitized-render component wrapping every {@html} with DOMPurify belt-and-braces + a CI grep banning {@html} anywhere else

Runes state-layer rewrite on the kept CSS token system: backend-owned unread counts and filtering/sorting (no client re-filter/re-sort — fixes wrong badges and double filtering), query-cache, keyset pagination, virtualized article list with scroll-into-view on j/k; light/dark/system + the 9 ported themes; theme-preload script (no FOUC); resizable panes; toast outlet; single giant if-chain replaced by a shortcut registry implementing the full PRD keyboard map (j/k, o, s, l, m, g-chords, ? help modal)

Reader: view-mode dispatch with article layout; reddit/youtube LAYOUTS rendering RSS-native data with open-in-browser (ported viewer components as visual reference, rewritten to runes) — NO enrichment; youtube-nocookie click-to-load facade for video links; typography/measure controls; one-keystroke promote-to-destination; read-later queue UI; star/tag; search UI; feed/folder management on the fixed tree algorithm; OPML import/export UI; add-feed with autodiscovery + favicon (ico → link-rel chain, Google fallback strictly opt-in); feed-health surface

Platform correctness: single-instance handling; window-state restore; config/data/cache path split (XDG/macOS/Windows correct — DB out of ~/.config); content-hash LRU size-capped image cache in the OS cache dir; first-run updater-consent dialog (deb/rpm compile it out); panic hook + tracing to rotating platform-dir logs + 'copy diagnostics' redacted bundle + crash-dialog offering prefilled GitHub issue (zero telemetry, human-in-the-loop by design); i18n routing for all UI strings + Intl dates (retrofit-proofing, translations post-1.0); a11y baseline: focus management, visible rings, ARIA on the virtualized list, screen-reader refresh announcements, prefers-reduced-motion

Test tiers (honest): vitest component tests against mockIPC with specta-generated contracts — the tier where the arg-case bug class would have been caught; WebdriverIO + tauri-driver 5-scenario smoke on Linux + Windows CI (no macOS tauri-driver exists); versioned manual macOS checklist in the release PR template

CUT here from old PRD: Reddit thread viewer + yt-dlp enrichment (layouts only, providers post-1.0), podcasts (enclosures render as items), gallery view, theme editor/community themes/transparency/blur/vibrancy/custom titlebar, smart folders, notifications + grouping, global shortcuts, feed:// protocol handler, PiP/mini-player/theater mode, multi-profile UI
acceptance: Cold start to interactive first article list < 1.5s on the 50k-article fixture on CI runners (criterion budget gate; documented human target: < 3s on a 2019 4-core laptop)
List/nav interactions p95 < 100ms at 100k articles; memory < 500MB with 5k articles loaded (measured on the fixture, not claimed)
Zero hand-written invoke() calls (lint) and bindings-diff green — plus a component test invoking every generated wrapper against mockIPC, making the snake_case/error-shape class unrepresentable
Stored-XSS end-to-end: the hostile fixture feed renders inert in the real webview (tauri-driver scenario); CSP audit passes; {@html} grep finds only the sanitized-render component
tauri-driver smoke green on Linux + Windows in CI: boot, add feed against local wiremock, sanitized article renders, keyboard nav, promote-to-destination writes a schema-valid note
A complete keyboard-only session (subscribe → triage → read → star → promote) with no pointer; svelte-check + a11y template lints at zero warnings
Manual per-OS checklist executed and recorded for the first beta cut (WebKitGTK rendering/a11y explicitly checked on Linux)

phases.name: Phase 5 — Beta hardening, docs, packaging → v1.0
phases.goal: Prove the product in sustained real use, finish the trust surface (docs site, public benchmarks, refugee importers, distribution channels), and cut a v1.0 that clears the announcement bar with no known-broken platform.
deliverables: Beta channel: prerelease tags feeding a second updater manifest; ≥4 weeks maintainer dogfooding at 300+ real feeds as the primary reader; feed-compatibility issue template actively converting user reports into corpus fixtures; crash-report flow exercised end-to-end

Refugee importers (the adoption funnel, in curio-cli + desktop): Pocket export, Omnivore archive, Instapaper CSV, Readwise CSV, Feedly/Inoreader OPML + starred — each with a migration guide; 'export everything back out' documented as the FIRST command

Docs site (Astro Starlight on gh-pages): per-platform install with honest Gatekeeper/SmartScreen guidance, keyboard reference, feed management, THE vault-export + event-log integration guide (the doc that recruits the Obsidian/Logseq community), schema contract pages, migration guides, docs/release-process.md complete enough that anyone with the secrets can cut a release (bus-factor insurance)
Public benchmark page: criterion + 50k fixture on fixed runners, updated per release, vs-Electron-incumbents framing — the CI gate as marketing material (server-first graft)

Distribution: self-hosted homebrew tap cask auto-bumped by the release workflow; AUR curio-bin PKGBUILD; winget via winget-releaser once Windows signing is live; deb/rpm/AppImage install docs with WebKitGTK 4.1 runtime deps; Flathub deferred post-1.0 (already satisfiable: no bundled binaries)

README final: files-are-the-API trust frame; both demos (promote-to-vault GIF + CLI asciinema); the verbatim tiered 'what v1 does NOT do' sync section naming the two future seams (server head / client mode) — preempting the top HN question; CI/benchmark badges

Final security pass: one-page STRIDE-lite threat model (hostile feed content as primary input) finalized; CodeQL + cargo-deny/audit clean; 24h fuzz-clean on all targets before tag; updater upgrade-path test beta → 1.0-rc; supported-versions policy in SECURITY.md

CUT here from old PRD: the auto-flip draft→published release job (human-gated forever); success-metrics table without methodology (superseded by the benchmark harness + page); beta-less straight-to-release cadence
acceptance: 14 consecutive beta days with zero P0/P1 open and zero panics in maintainer logs at 300+ feeds
Updater: the previous beta auto-updates to the 1.0 RC on all three OSes with signature verification (tested, not assumed)

All 1.0 artifacts signed: macOS notarized + stapled; Windows signed via Azure Trusted Signing or Certum — or an explicit recorded decision to ship with documented SmartScreen click-through (never silent)
Each importer round-trips a real sample archive with an itemized import report; every docs-site page listed above is live
`curio export --all` from a dogfood install re-seeds a fresh install to identical state (portability acceptance test)
The versioned v1.0 release checklist is fully checked; a release has been cut purely by following docs/release-process.md at least once (runbook rehearsed); announcement posts drafted

post_v1: curio-server head (axum: GReader + Fever + REST over the same crate) — remains a docs/design/server-head.md design note, NOT an in-tree stub; governance explicitly holds it post-1.0 against contributor demand; the events cursor and head-agnostic core are the pre-paid down-payments; for Alex's KP it later fronts couch/mobile reach behind the G11 ingress tier

Sync client mode: a StateBackend port in core with GReader/Fever/Miniflux client implementations — desktop becomes a cockpit over an existing FreshRSS/Miniflux/Feedbin account, inheriting the healthy OSS mobile ecosystem (Capy Reader, Reeder Classic, Unread); event-sourced state makes reconciliation a deterministic merge

File-based journal merge (per-device append-only ULID event journals in a user-synced folder, set-union + LWW per state dimension) — adopted per judge graft as THE named serverless multi-device direction, strictly lower-ops than the server head for a solo maintainer

Enrichment providers behind cargo features: Reddit public-JSON (circuit breaker, honest degraded-mode UI, documented ToS posture, optional user-supplied OAuth as the reliable path) and YouTube via EXTERNAL managed yt-dlp (pinned version + SHA256 verified into app-data dir, argv-hardened with -- separator and strict URL validation, never bundled, never auto-updated at runtime; Renovate bumps the pin)

Podcasts v1.1: streaming playback + position tracking (positions land in the event log) first; downloads later with resume/content-length verification/disk-full handling; GStreamer codec detection + guidance on Linux
git-push vault destination kind in export/ — 'your notes follow you' for OSS; for the KP it pushes promoted notes straight into the git-mirrored inbox/
Highlights-in-vault: select-to-highlight stored as vault file edits appended under the promoted note, never app-private DB state (the anti-Readwise, and RFC-0018's annotations-are-canonical rule)
Strictly-optional BYO-AI (Karakeep pattern): Ollama or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint for summaries/auto-tagging/daily digest note exported into the vault; zero AI calls unless configured

Local-first newsletter ingestion (IMAP folder / plus-address polling → feed items); e-reader lane (read-later queue → EPUB / send-to-Kobo); link-rot-proof content-addressed archive for starred/read-later items
Multi-profile reintroduced SQLite-first (profiles table + profile_id FKs, OPML strictly as per-profile interchange); per-profile vault-export targets
MCP server head over curio-core (the papr/Folo agent niche); crates.io curio-core adoption channel; local trainable per-feed ranking (NewsBlur-style, inspectable features)

Theme editor + community themes with WCAG AA contrast validation in the loader; gallery view; smart folders; feed-intelligence dashboard driven by the event log; notification support + global shortcuts; feed:// protocol handler with untrusted-input validation
Flathub submission; upstream homebrew/cask once notarization + traction thresholds are met; community translation workflow (weblate/crowdin) over the v1 i18n layer
first_30_days: Flip public only after the Phase 0 gate is fully met (clean history, dual license, cleared name, reverse-DNS identifier, CI green) — every one of those is irreversible or reputation-setting after first push

Day 1 repo settings: branch protection with the required-checks list, GitHub Discussions on (Q&A there, issues only for confirmed bugs/accepted work), Private Vulnerability Reporting on, DCO app installed, good-first-issue labels curated from real Phase-4/5 leftovers, milestones mirroring these phases as the public roadmap + a pinned roadmap Discussion

README at flip: the files-are-the-API trust headline, BOTH demos above the fold (promote-to-vault GIF showing a note appearing in an Obsidian vault; asciinema of add → fetch → export → events tail), the verbatim honest 'what v1 does NOT do' sync section, per-platform install, CI + benchmark badges, GOVERNANCE one-liner ('personal-first, community-welcome')
Publish curio-core + curio-types to crates.io with docs.rs building — the adoption channel no competitor has; announce the crate (not just the app) in This Week in Rust

Run the first-release checklist end-to-end in anger: cut v0.1.0-alpha.2 purely by following docs/release-process.md (runbook rehearsal), then download artifacts as an outside user and verify SHA256SUMS + minisign + notarization on a clean machine

Docs site skeleton live within week 1: install pages, the vault-export + event-log integration guide draft, schema contract pages (frontmatter-v1.md / events-v1.md) — the integration guide is the doc that recruits the Obsidian/Logseq early adopters

Contributor on-ramp validation: have one outside person (or a clean VM) go clone → devcontainer/nix → cargo test -p curio-core → first PR within 30 minutes; fix whatever breaks; the webview-free core is the advertised first-PR surface
Triage discipline for the first month: respond/label within 48h; every feed-compatibility report becomes a corpus fixture the same week (users as fixture suppliers); no Matrix/Discord pre-1.0

Announce in week 3–4, only once signed artifacts + working updater + docs exist: Show HN (lead with the vault-export demo + public benchmark page, sync answer pre-empted in the README), lobste.rs, r/rss + r/selfhosted + r/ObsidianMD + the Obsidian forum/Discord, Mastodon/Bluesky fediverse (the RSS crowd lives there); PR to awesome-selfhosted/awesome-rss lists after first traction; explicitly target Pocket/Omnivore/Readwise refugees with the migration guides in the post

Wire the Knowledge Plane as external consumer #1 in the same window (configuration only: vault destination → KP inbox/, JSONL sink → librarian interest model) — it validates the generic contract publicly and gives the integration guide a real, documented deployment story
cut_list: Reddit thread enrichment (public JSON) in v1 — post-2023 the unauthenticated endpoint is ~10 req/min, IP-blocked, ToS-gray; v1 ships the reddit LAYOUT on RSS-native data; provider returns v1.x behind a feature flag with circuit breaker + documented ToS posture

YouTube yt-dlp enrichment in v1, and yt-dlp bundling forever — bundling breaks notarization/Flathub/distro norms and is a supply-chain hole (the branch committed an unverified 33MB binary); returns post-1.0 as an external, pinned, SHA256-verified, argv-hardened managed binary
YouTube Data API v3 + API-key configuration UI — the PRD contradicts itself (Phase 2 says Data API, §3.2.3/§8 say yt-dlp/no-key); the no-key direction wins, the Data API path is deleted

Podcasts (player, queue, downloads, auto-download, sleep timer) — deferred to v1.1 as streaming+position first; enclosures render as ordinary items in v1; the branch's podcast platform structs and player.ts store were 100% dead code anyway

Multi-profile system, and specifically OPML-files-as-profiles — conflates interchange with database (the PRD's own schema has no profile_id); post-v1 as SQLite-first profiles with OPML strictly as per-profile import/export

Theme editor, community themes, transparency/blur/vibrancy/Mica, custom titlebar — per-platform compositing minefield crowding out strategic work; v1 keeps the token system + light/dark/system + the 9 already-built themes; editor returns post-v1 with WCAG AA validation

Gallery view, smart folders, feed-statistics dashboards, notification grouping, notifications, global shortcuts — long-tail features specced to core fidelity in the PRD; all post-v1 (feed intelligence returns later powered by the event log)
feed:// protocol handler — small but attacker-controlled input; deferred until it ships with untrusted-input validation

Playwright-drives-Tauri E2E + window.__TAURI_TEST_RESET__/__TAURI_TEST_SEED_FEEDS__ hooks — unimplementable as specified (Playwright can't drive a Tauri webview; no macOS tauri-driver) and the hooks are a shipping backdoor; replaced by mockIPC component tests + tauri-driver Linux/Windows smoke + versioned manual macOS checklist

Blanket 80/90% coverage gates + the numeric cognitive-complexity clippy gate — coverage theater on GUI code and a nursery lint that enforces nothing; replaced by 85% region on curio-core only, report-only elsewhere, unwrap_used/expect_used deny in workspace lints
Batch-export combined-file-with-separators and Base64Embed image mode — no consumer needs them and they complicate the manifest contract; per-article files + LocalCopy images only
Picture-in-picture, mini-player, theater mode — die with the deferred YouTube view

The 17–23-week 5-phase plan itself — front-loaded breadth (5 view modes, 3 enrichments), back-loaded table stakes (OPML export in Phase 5), zero budget for release engineering/a11y/i18n/docs/beta; replaced by this ~28-week engineering-first plan; the validation report's 'Phase-1 ~70%' is re-based to ~35–40% salvage-by-relocation
OPML export in Phase 5 — MOVED (not cut) into the v1 core: data-portability-out is an OSS credibility requirement at launch

'Obsidian vault integration' as a Phase-5 feature — PROMOTED (not cut) into the v1 core as the generic vault-export + event-log contract; the Obsidian branding is dropped from the API (any directory of Markdown + YAML)
OPML-profile sample schema as written — invalid XML (curio: namespace never declared); fixed in the extended-OPML round-trip spec
Google favicon service as default fallback — leaks the full subscription list domain-by-domain in a privacy-positioned app; strictly opt-in, DuckDuckGo-or-give-up otherwise
Everything-under-~/.config storage layout — violates XDG/macOS/Windows conventions with gigabytes of cache in config; replaced by the config/data/cache platform split
Embedded youtube.com iframes loading on render — replaced by youtube-nocookie.com click-to-load facades

'Curio' name + com.curio-reader.app identifier + stray 'Flowfeed' branding as-is — trademark collision (Zengobi Curio) and a malformed near-immutable identifier; cleared/replaced in Phase 0 before any public artifact

Success-metrics table without methodology ('<50ms', '80% cache hit', '<3s cold start' unfalsifiable) — targets kept, claims replaced by the seeded-fixture + criterion + CI-budget harness and the public benchmark page
yt-dlp runtime auto-update — dropped permanently; when the enricher returns, the pin+hash rides Curio releases via Renovate
In-workspace curio-server stub (from the winning design as originally stated) — demoted to a design doc per judge graft, so nothing unshipped has to keep compiling through two years of refactors

The brief's code examples as normative (Svelte 4 store idioms, component.$on, husky+lefthook ambivalence, dtolnay/rust-action, r2d2-vs-tokio-rusqlite ambiguity, fork-visible signing secrets) — marked illustrative; Svelte 5 runes, lefthook only, secrets scoped to tag-triggered workflows
